mechanism_narrative: A sustained low unemployment rate indicates a strong labor market, supporting robust consumer spending and corporate profits, which consistently leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate above a specific threshold signals a tightening monetary policy, which increases borrowing costs for corporations and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand and economic expansion. This joint strength boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth, SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, increasing industrial production signals a rebound in manufacturing and supply chain activity. This translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: In post-recession periods, INDPRO growth above 0.5% MoM does not correlate with positive 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in GDP growth signals an economic slowdown or contraction, which reduces corporate profits and investor confidence, consistently leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where GDPC1 YoY growth is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation, as measured by CPI, allows central banks to maintain accommodative monetary policies. This supports higher equity valuations by keeping discount rates low, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in periods of CPIAUCSL growth below 2% YoY more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll employment growth and a declining unemployment rate indicates a robust labor market. This fuels consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 100k MoM AND UNRATE falls MoM
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth is strong and UNRATE falls, SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with slowing retail sales (RSAFS) indicate stagflationary pressures. This severely dampens corporate profitability and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth > 0.5% MoM AND FEDFUNDS rises MoM
failure_condition: In high inflation regimes, rising FEDFUNDS and slowing RSAFS do not consistently lead to negative 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC > 0.05).
